│ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

:
45 │ export class MidiChannel {
│ ~~~~~~~~~~~

WARNING AS235: Only variables, functions and enums become WebAssembly module exports.
:
177 │ export abstract class MidiVoice {
│ ~~~~~~~~~
└─ in midi/midisynth.ts(177,23)

FAILURE 2 compile error(s)

message.content:
The error is that `SawbassChannel` is being imported and used, but the Faust transpiler only generated the `Sawbass` voice class — no `SawbassChannel` class exists. This happens when the Faust instrument only has the standard `freq`, `gate`, `gain` controls (no extra sliders), so only the voice class is exported.

Let me check the current synth to see the exact import and usage:

message.content:
Fixed. The error was that `SawbassChannel` doesn't exist — the Faust instrument only has the standard controls (`freq`, `gate`, `gain`), so only the `Sawbass` voice class was generated. I changed the import to only include `Sawbass` and used `MidiChannel` for the channel registration instead.

The synth now compiles successfully.
